channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 27th Sunday after Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 27th Sunday after Pentecost

item.title: 27th Sunday after Pentecost, The Healing Of The Woman With An Infirmity Of Eighteen Years, It is really pretty simple., 2008-12-21
item.description: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The Healing Of The Woman With An Infirmity Of Eighteen Years, It is really pretty simple., 2008-12-21
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The Healing Of The Woman With An Infirmity Of Eighteen Years, It is really pretty simple., 2008-12-21

item.title: 27th Sunday after Pentecost, Healing Of The Woman With An Infirmity Of Eighteen Years, 2009-12-13
item.description: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. Healing Of The Woman With An Infirmity Of Eighteen Years, 2009-12-13
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. Healing Of The Woman With An Infirmity Of Eighteen Years, 2009-12-13

item.title: 27th Sunday after Pentecost, Jesus Christ Came Into The World To Save Sinners, What Is Sin, 2010-12-12
item.description: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The epistle read today succinctly sums up the reason for the incarnation of the God-man: "Jesus Christ came into the world to save sinners". To understand this, we must understand what sin is. Most have a great misconception about sin and only think that is is something that offends Almighty God, and do not understand that the reason God is offended is because all sin darkens us, and makes us "bent over", unable to see God and know Him. The effect of sin is well shown by the woman with the infirmity of 18 years. Lets us explore what sin does to us, and the only solution to our terrible predicament.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The epistle read today succinctly sums up the reason for the incarnation of the God-man: "Jesus Christ came into the world to save sinners". To understand this, we must understand what sin is. Most have a great misconception about sin and only think that is is something that offends Almighty God, and do not understand that the reason God is offended is because all sin darkens us, and makes us "bent over", unable to see God and know Him. The effect of sin is well shown by the woman with the infirmity of 18 years. Lets us explore what sin does to us, and the only solution to our terrible predicament.

item.title: 27th Sunday after Pentecost, 2012-12-09
item.description: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The healing of the woman with a spirit of infirmity of 18 years warns us to not be formal in our religion. Love is the highest law; we can only obey this law if we understand something critically important about ourselves. This healing teaches us both of these things.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The healing of the woman with a spirit of infirmity of 18 years warns us to not be formal in our religion. Love is the highest law; we can only obey this law if we understand something critically important about ourselves. This healing teaches us both of these things.

item.title: 27th Sunday after Pentecost, A Woman Which Had A Spirit Of Infirmity Eighteen Years And Was Bowed Together And Could In No Wise Lift Up Herself, 2013-12-08
item.description: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The Gospel is a mirror for our souls. We should learn something about ourselves every time we read it. The sinful and cruel reaction of the rule of the synagogue when the woman with an infirmity of 18 years was healed on the Sabbath has much to teach us - about ourselves. If we do not know ourselves, we will not love God. This Gospel will teach us, and teach us how to become more compassionate, if we have the humility to listen.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :27th Sunday after Pentecost. The Gospel is a mirror for our souls. We should learn something about ourselves every time we read it. The sinful and cruel reaction of the rule of the synagogue when the woman with an infirmity of 18 years was healed on the Sabbath has much to teach us - about ourselves. If we do not know ourselves, we will not love God. This Gospel will teach us, and teach us how to become more compassionate, if we have the humility to listen.
